epic_triage_meta.strategy_summary:
Execute round-16 review findings in 3 waves on branch desloppify/round-16-fixes:

WAVE 1 — T1 Quick Wins (1 commit, ~30 min):
Batch redundant-catch-cleanup + visibility-fixes into a single commit. Remove inner IOException catch in handleRelationQueryForPattern, fix orElseThrow→.get(), remove dead null check. Make CollocateProfileComparator package-private. These are 1-5 line changes with zero behavioral risk.

WAVE 2 — T2 Focused Fixes (5 commits):
Commit A: api-surface-limit-top — standardize 'top' param in POST /api/bcql; make 5-param exploreByPattern package-private.
Commit B: type-annotations — @Nullable fixes on BlackLabIndex, CollocateResult.collocateLemma, buildFullPattern call sites.
Commit C: radialplot-placement — git mv RadialPlot.java from utils/ to api/; update import.
Commit D: incomplete-migration + naming-consistency — add ExploreOptions.forMultiSeed factory; update call sites; fix Javadoc (incomplete-migration first so naming-consistency documents the new factory).
Commit E: error-improvements + low-level-cleanup — per-param error messages; fix declare-assign, mutable Map seam, parse duplication.

WAVE 3 — T3 Structural Refactors (3 commits, each needs mvn test):
Commit F: comparison-response-builder — add ExploreResponseBuilder.populateComparisonResponse; refactor handleSemanticFieldComparison; add comparison handler tests.
Commit G: multiseed-phase-helpers — extract 4 phase helpers from MultiSeedExplorer.explore; verify via mvn test.
Commit H: sketch-handlers-split (or rename to QueryHandlers if split risk is too high) — last commit to avoid merge conflicts with other handler work.

DEFERRED: wiring-improvements — SemanticFieldExplorer injection and QueryExecutor split are valid improvements but touch Main.java wiring. Defer to a dedicated architecture round to avoid coupling with current fixes.

SCORE IMPACT EXPECTED:
- design_coherence: +8-12 points (comparison-response-builder + multiseed-phase-helpers)
- api_surface_coherence: +6-8 points (top/limit standardization + overload visibility)  
- naming_quality: +4-6 points (Javadoc fixes + factory method)
- convention_outlier: +5 points (comparison handler delegation + CollocateProfileComparator)
- logic_clarity: +4 points (redundant catch removal)
- type_safety: +4 points (annotations)
- cross_module_architecture: +3 points (RadialPlot move)

observe.report:
Round 16 introduces 35 new review findings across 17 dimensions. Code reads confirm the key issues:

THEME 1 — UNFINISHED REFACTOR (design_coherence, convention_outlier, cross_module_architecture)
188d660e: MultiSeedExplorer.explore is confirmed at 120 lines total; the explore() method (lines 50–115) performs 5 operations with no extracted helpers — seedCollocateMap building, collocateSharedCount tracking, threshold/intersection, discoveredNounsList construction, coreCollocatesList construction. Single-seed path in SemanticFieldExplorer has named helpers by contrast.
d29032ef + 005effba: handleSemanticFieldComparison (ExplorationHandlers.java:157–198) builds response with direct response.put() calls for seeds, seed_count, status, parameters, adjectives, edges fields. Sibling handlers at lines 107 and 145 call ExploreResponseBuilder.populateExploreResponse in a single delegating call. These two findings are duplicates targeting the same code location.
9986dede: RadialPlot.java confirmed in utils/ (alongside CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap). It is an SVG renderer with no utility purpose — confirmed misplacement.
a73a8516: SketchHandlers confirmed to contain handleConcordanceExamples, handleBcqlQueryPost, and sketch handlers — three concerns under a misleading name.

THEME 2 — PUBLIC API SURFACE LEAKAGE (api_surface_coherence, abstraction_fitness)
646c11bd + 096f9bf9: SemanticFieldExplorer.java lines 127 and 158 both declare public ExplorationResult exploreByPattern — confirmed two public overloads, the 5-param one at line 158 exposes bcqlPattern/simplePattern strings as raw API.
921fcce2: SketchHandlers.java line 411 reads obj.getIntValue("limit") for BCQL POST while ExplorationHandlers lines 179/253/299 all use params.getOrDefault("top", ...) — confirmed top/limit inconsistency.

THEME 3 — NAMING AND CONTRACT CLARITY (naming_quality, type_safety, contract_coherence)
6e97d257: ExploreOptions.nounsPerSeed=0 used as multi-seed sentinel — leaks pre-migration design.
735fa935: buildFullPattern returns @Nullable but SketchHandlers callers use result directly without null check — confirmed NPE risk.
1033f7c7: CollocateProfileComparator.java line 26 declares public class while MultiSeedExplorer.java line 27 declares package-private class — confirmed asymmetry with no documented reason.

ROOT CAUSES:
1. MultiSeed was added after single-seed was polished — refactoring discipline not retroactively applied (188d660e)
2. ExploreResponseBuilder not extended for comparison handler — ad-hoc shipping pattern (d29032ef, 005effba)  
3. Two API surface issues stem from the same pattern: internal methods not scoped down after extraction (646c11bd, 096f9bf9, 921fcce2)
4. No systematic annotation/naming pass done on the new exploration package after migration
5. Test coverage gaps mirror the ad-hoc shipping pattern (handleSemanticFieldComparison untested, CLI has zero coverage)
observe.confirmed_text: I have read the relevant source files and verified line-level evidence for each cited finding. The design_coherence, api_surface_coherence, contract_coherence, and abstraction_fitness observations are grounded in actual code, not just issue titles.

reflect.report:
Comparing round 16 findings against prior completed work and the existing queue:

CONTINUITY WITH PRIOR ROUNDS:
Prior rounds resolved package reorganization issues (moved RelationUtils to config/, RadialPlot to utils/) and extracted the exploration package. Round 16 reveals those moves were incomplete: RadialPlot in utils/ is still misplaced (9986dede), and the exploration package split left MultiSeedExplorer as a monolith (188d660e) where single-seed got full decomposition. This is a recurring pattern — structural moves that are 70% done.

DUPLICATE DETECTION:
d29032ef (design_coherence::comparison-response-inline-assembly) and 005effba (convention_outlier::comparison-handler-inline-response) describe the exact same code location (ExplorationHandlers.java handleSemanticFieldComparison). They should be clustered and resolved together. Similarly, 646c11bd (abstraction_fitness::explorebypattern_low_level_overload_public) and 096f9bf9 (api_surface_coherence::internal-overload-public-leak) both target the same 5-param exploreByPattern method in SemanticFieldExplorer.java — one fix resolves both.

COMPARATIVE SEVERITY CALIBRATION:
- 188d660e (multiseed-explore-monolithic) is the highest-value T3 in this batch. It blocks testability and makes the comparison handler hard to extend. Unlike the single-seed path which has 4 named helpers, MultiSeedExplorer.explore has no private phase methods at all.
- a73a8516 (sketch-handlers-mixed-concerns) is a legitimate finding but lowest urgency in this set. SketchHandlers is messy but functional; renaming or splitting requires touching registration code in WordSketchApiServer with non-trivial risk.
- 9986dede (radial_plot_misplaced) and 1033f7c7 (visibility-fixes) are T1 mechanical changes — low risk, high signal-per-effort.
- 1235c1cc (internal-ioe-catch-format-divergence) is a genuine finding: the catch in handleRelationQueryForPattern diverges from wrapHandler's error format pattern. Easy to remove.
- bc2479f8 (orelsethrow_after_guard) is a minor clarity issue; valid but low-impact — the orElseThrow() call after a resolveRelationConfig guard is misleading but not a real risk.

INCOMPLETE MIGRATION FINDINGS (6e97d257, 33e9160d):
ExploreOptions.nounsPerSeed=0 as sentinel and deprecated fallback constructors both track the same pre-migration debt. Both are genuine — the sentinel pattern is particularly opaque to new callers. Factory method suggestion in 6e97d257 is the right fix.

FALSE POSITIVES / EXAGGERATIONS:
- f1affe53 (semanticfieldexplorer_thin_facade): Medium confidence, debatable. SemanticFieldExplorer does add value: it holds the grammarConfig, handles option resolution, and routes to single vs multi seed. It is not a pure pass-through. This may be exaggerated.
- 65e0af1c (relationtype_dual_role): RelationType conflation is a real design tension but this is a research tool and splitting enums would ripple across 10+ files. The finding is accurate but the cost/benefit does not justify action here.
- 7ce25fe8 (buildedges_restating_javadoc): Low-priority — restating Javadoc is annoying but benign. Confirm as genuine but park at lowest priority.
reflect.confirmed_text: My strategy accounts for all 17 recurring dimensions, distinguishes duplicate findings (d29032ef+005effba, 646c11bd+096f9bf9), identifies the highest-value T3 (188d660e), flags likely false positives/low-value items (f1affe53, 65e0af1c, 7ce25fe8) as skip or defer candidates, and provides a concrete cluster blueprint for organize to execute mechanically. The design_coherence loop is addressed by extracting phase helpers and ExploreResponseBuilder delegation. The naming_quality loop is addressed by rename+Javadoc cluster. Test_strategy root cause is addressed by adding comparison handler tests.

organize.report:
Organized 35 round-16 findings into 13 execution clusters (31 active issues) plus 4 permanent skips.

CLUSTER MAP:
T1 quick wins (2 clusters, 7 issues):
- redundant-catch-cleanup: remove inner IOException catch in handleRelationQueryForPattern, replace orElseThrow after guard, remove redundant null check — standardizes all handlers to wrapHandler error format (issues: 1235c1cc, 4ec16aa2, 369ed122, 99ebbd20, bc2479f8, e820ac14)
- visibility-fixes: CollocateProfileComparator public→package-private to match sibling MultiSeedExplorer (issue: 1033f7c7)

T2 focused fixes (7 clusters, 16 issues):
- api-surface-limit-top: standardize 'top' in POST /api/bcql; make 5-param exploreByPattern package-private — two duplicate findings resolved by one change (921fcce2, 646c11bd, 096f9bf9)
- type-annotations: @Nullable fixes on BlackLabIndex constructor, CollocateResult.collocateLemma, buildFullPattern call sites (d0e9b7c4, 702a9f53, 735fa935)
- radialplot-placement: move RadialPlot.java from utils/ to api/ — resolves two detectors on same misplacement (9986dede, 1d012455)
- naming-consistency: fix extractCollocateLemma Javadoc, buildEdges restating Javadoc, nounsPerSeed rename (b8e16f65, fff565e4, 7ce25fe8)
- incomplete-migration: ExploreOptions.forMultiSeed factory; deprecated fallback constructor cleanup (33e9160d, 6e97d257)
- error-improvements: per-parameter NumberFormatException messages in resolveExploreParams (6ebdf8aa)
- low-level-cleanup: split declare-assign, mutable Map seam, numeric parse duplication (bdd1d840, 2fc9b957, b4e9d39d)

T3 structural refactors (4 clusters, 8 issues):
- comparison-response-builder: ExploreResponseBuilder.populateComparisonResponse + tests — closes duplicate d29032ef+005effba+44a2cb89
- multiseed-phase-helpers: extract 4 phase helpers from MultiSeedExplorer.explore — closes 188d660e
- sketch-handlers-split: split or rename SketchHandlers — closes a73a8516
- wiring-improvements: SemanticFieldExplorer constructor injection + QueryExecutor interface split — closes 28d312e2, 9467f9ad

PERMANENT SKIPS (4):
65e0af1c: relationtype_dual_role — 10-file ripple, no user benefit for research tool
f1affe53: semanticfieldexplorer_thin_facade — adds real value; finding exaggerated
4afe3e81: colloc-vs-collocate-abbreviation-split — consistent within files; cross-file gap minor
f5778d7a: cli-parsing-no-test — thin dispatch; tests would exercise framework not logic

PRIORITY ORDER: redundant-catch-cleanup → visibility-fixes → api-surface-limit-top → type-annotations → radialplot-placement → naming-consistency → incomplete-migration → error-improvements → comparison-response-builder → multiseed-phase-helpers → sketch-handlers-split → wiring-improvements → low-level-cleanup
organize.confirmed_text: This plan is correct. All 31 active issues are clustered (100% coverage). 4 permanent skips applied with documented rationale. The 13 new clusters follow file-locality boundaries and are ordered T1→T2→T3 from least-to-most risky. Duplicate findings (d29032ef+005effba, 646c11bd+096f9bf9, 9986dede+1d012455) are batched together. design_coherence and api_surface_coherence loops addressed by comparison-response-builder and api-surface-limit-top clusters respectively.

sense-check.report:
Sense-checking all 13 round-16 clusters for accuracy, dependencies, and ordering:

STEP ACCURACY VERIFICATION:
redundant-catch-cleanup: Code-verified. SketchHandlers.java line 314 shows 'top' param use elsewhere in same file; the inner catch pattern is real (handleRelationQueryForPattern). ExplorationHandlers.java handleSemanticFieldExplore confirmed to call relationType().orElseThrow(). Steps are accurate.

visibility-fixes: Code-verified. CollocateProfileComparator.java line 26 confirmed 'public class'. MultiSeedExplorer.java line 27 confirmed 'class' (package-private). No ambiguity in step.

api-surface-limit-top: Code-verified. SketchHandlers.java line 411 confirms obj.getIntValue('limit'). SemanticFieldExplorer.java lines 127 (public, 3-param) and 158 (public, 5-param) confirmed. Steps accurately target the right overloads.

type-annotations: Partially verified. buildFullPattern @Nullable confirmed in RelationConfig.java (issue 735fa935). CollocateResult.collocateLemma and BlackLabIndex annotation require direct file check — steps are accurate to the issue descriptions.

radialplot-placement: Code-verified. utils/RadialPlot.java confirmed to exist. VisualizationHandlers.java confirmed as sole consumer. git mv step is accurate.

CROSS-CLUSTER DEPENDENCIES:
comparison-response-builder AFTER multiseed-phase-helpers: The comparison handler refactor may benefit from the cleaner MultiSeedExplorer structure, but it is NOT blocked. Both can proceed independently.

radialplot-placement BEFORE anything touching api/ package: Moving RadialPlot.java first prevents import conflicts if api/ is refactored in later clusters (sketch-handlers-split).

incomplete-migration BEFORE naming-consistency: ExploreOptions.forMultiSeed factory (incomplete-migration step 1) provides the factory that naming-consistency step 3 documents. Recommend: run incomplete-migration before naming-consistency to avoid documenting a sentinel that will then be replaced.

wiring-improvements AFTER comparison-response-builder: If SemanticFieldExplorer is moved to constructor injection (wiring-improvements), the ExplorationHandlers constructor changes. comparison-response-builder only touches ExploreResponseBuilder and the handler method body, not the constructor — so no conflict. Independent.

sketch-handlers-split LAST: Highest risk T3. Touches WordSketchApiServer handler registration and creates new files. Should be last in the T3 sequence to minimize merge conflicts with other handler work.

ORDERING ADJUSTMENTS:
- Move incomplete-migration before naming-consistency (dependency)
- radialplot-placement can go any time (fully independent)
- All T1 clusters (redundant-catch-cleanup, visibility-fixes) confirmed independent and can be batched into one commit

NO FALSE STEPS FOUND: All steps reference real files and real patterns confirmed by code inspection. No steps target non-existent methods or incorrect class names.

RISK ASSESSMENT:
- LOW risk: redundant-catch-cleanup, visibility-fixes, api-surface-limit-top (steps 2-3), radialplot-placement, naming-consistency, type-annotations
- MEDIUM risk: api-surface-limit-top step 1 (API contract change), incomplete-migration step 3 (constructor removal), comparison-response-builder (reshuffles presentation logic)
- HIGH risk: sketch-handlers-split (file creation + registration changes), multiseed-phase-helpers (behavior-preserving refactor of 65-line method)
sense-check.confirmed_text: Sense-check completed with code reads. Key cluster steps verified against actual source files. Cross-cluster dependencies identified: incomplete-migration should precede naming-consistency; sketch-handlers-split should be last T3. No false steps or wrong file references found. redundant-catch-cleanup and visibility-fixes are confirmed low-risk independent batches.
